description_long: This chair is sculpted in a highly stylized, organic form resembling a large, roughly carved hand with three prominent, rounded prongs or fingers rising from a thick base. The surface is uneven and textured, with visible creases and folds that mimic the look of crumpled paper or weathered stone. The coloration is a mottled beige with darker brown and blackish shading in the recesses, giving it a worn, earthy appearance. The seat area is indented between the prongs, suggesting a place to sit, while the back and sides curve upward to provide some support. There are no visible legs or traditional chair features; the entire structure appears monolithic and solid. This unique design would stand out as a piece of urban art or unconventional street furniture.
description: A stylized, hand-shaped chair with three thick, rounded prongs and a textured, mottled beige surface. The seat is indented and the form is monolithic, resembling a sculpted block.
description_view_0: Front view shows three prominent prongs and a deep central indentation forming the seat area.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thick, rounded prongs and the curved, organic profile of the chair.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the solid, blocky back and the separation between the prongs.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the uneven, sculpted surface and the upward curve of the prongs.
